title: Athyrium flexile
taxonomy: Athyrium flexile is classified as a species of fern in the family Woodsiaceae, native to North America. The genus Athyrium comprises about 140 species of ferns. Athyrium flexile is a type of flexible lady fern, known for its delicate fronds. This plant is often found growing in moist, wooded areas, particularly in the eastern United States. Its classification is also sometimes associated with the family Dryopteridaceae, although the most recent classifications place it in the family Woodsiaceae.
uses: Athyrium flexile, also known as flexible lady fern, has been traditionally used in medicine due to its potential health benefits. intro: Athyrium flexile, also known as flexible lady fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Athyriaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help to reduce symptoms of arthritis, improve skin health, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Athyrium flexile include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
